info.title: Instantly Service
info.description: Cold email outreach service via Instantly.ai API. Manages campaigns, leads, accounts, analytics, and webhook events.

degraded.description: True when bronze or silver write failed but webhook was acknowledged with 200 to avoid Instantly auto-pause

degradedReason.description: Failure message when degraded=true, null otherwise

subject.description: Shared subject for all steps in the sequence

daysSinceLastStep.description: Delay in days since the previous step (0 = immediate)

sequence.description: Ordered email steps — at least one required

startedAt.description: ISO timestamp when the job was dispatched

contacted.description: Leads added to a campaign / send attempted

sent.description: Leads with at least 1 email sent (COUNT DISTINCT lead)

opened.description: Leads who opened at least 1 email

clicked.description: Leads who clicked at least 1 link

notSending.description: Leads in campaigns currently flagged with Instantly's not_sending_status diagnostic. Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

cancelled.description: Leads whose campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job (delivery_status='cancelled'). Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

opened.description: Unique emails opened at least once (COUNT DISTINCT)

clicked.description: Unique emails with at least 1 click (COUNT DISTINCT)

stepStats.description: Per-step breakdown (instantly broadcast only; empty/omitted for postmark transactional)

key.description: Group key. For groupBy=day this is YYYY-MM-DD in the requested timezone.

contacted.description: Leads added to a campaign / send attempted

sent.description: Leads with at least 1 email sent (COUNT DISTINCT lead)

opened.description: Leads who opened at least 1 email

clicked.description: Leads who clicked at least 1 link

notSending.description: Leads in campaigns currently flagged with Instantly's not_sending_status diagnostic. Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

cancelled.description: Leads whose campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job (delivery_status='cancelled'). Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

opened.description: Unique emails opened at least once (COUNT DISTINCT)

clicked.description: Unique emails with at least 1 click (COUNT DISTINCT)

stepStats.description: Per-step breakdown (instantly broadcast only; empty/omitted for postmark transactional)

workflowSlugs.description: Workflow slugs included in this aggregate.

averageMs.description: Average elapsed time in milliseconds. Null when sampleSize is 0.

medianMs.description: Median elapsed time in milliseconds. Null when sampleSize is 0.

sampleSize.description: Number of recipients included in the aggregate.

key.description: Caller-owned public group key, for example a workflow dynasty slug.

workflowSlugs.description: Workflow slugs included in this public-safe group.

contacted.description: Leads added to a campaign / send attempted

sent.description: Leads with at least 1 email sent (COUNT DISTINCT lead)

opened.description: Leads who opened at least 1 email

clicked.description: Leads who clicked at least 1 link

notSending.description: Leads in campaigns currently flagged with Instantly's not_sending_status diagnostic. Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

cancelled.description: Leads whose campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job (delivery_status='cancelled'). Optional in contract v1; will be required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

opened.description: Unique emails opened at least once (COUNT DISTINCT)

clicked.description: Unique emails with at least 1 click (COUNT DISTINCT)

stepStats.description: Per-step breakdown (instantly broadcast only; empty/omitted for postmark transactional)

sourceBrandId.description: Brand UUID to transfer from the source org

sourceOrgId.description: Current org UUID that owns the brand

targetBrandId.description: Brand UUID in the target org — when present, rewrites brand_id references to this value

idempotent.description: True if the latest existing row already matched the requested status — no new bronze row was inserted, no side effects fired

status.description: Manual reply qualification status — mirrors Instantly webhook reply event_type values exactly. Set by a human via the dashboard when Instantly fails to detect a reply (e.g. reply received on a non-leurre email account).

campaign_id.description: Logical campaign id (groups sub-campaigns for the same workflow run)

status.description: Manual reply qualification status — mirrors Instantly webhook reply event_type values exactly. Set by a human via the dashboard when Instantly fails to detect a reply (e.g. reply received on a non-leurre email account).

notes.description: Optional free-text human note for audit

example.notes: Reply received on Gmail — Instantly missed it

status.description: Manual reply qualification status — mirrors Instantly webhook reply event_type values exactly. Set by a human via the dashboard when Instantly fails to detect a reply (e.g. reply received on a non-leurre email account).

contacted.description: Whether at least one sending exists in this scope

sent.description: Whether an email was sent in this scope

delivered.description: Whether an email was delivered in this scope (sent AND not bounced)

opened.description: Whether the recipient opened any email in this scope

clicked.description: Whether the recipient clicked any link in this scope

replied.description: Whether the recipient replied in this scope (always false for postmark — no reply tracking)

replyClassification.description: Classification of most recent reply: positive / negative / neutral, or null if no reply (always null for postmark)

bounced.description: Whether an email bounced in this scope

unsubscribed.description: Whether the recipient unsubscribed in this scope

cancelled.description: Whether the campaign was cancelled by the retry-stuck job. Optional in contract v1; required in v2 after postmark padding ships.

lastDeliveredAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last delivery in this scope; null if none

firstContactedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a contacted event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstSentAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a sent event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstDeliveredAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a delivered event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstOpenedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of an opened event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstClickedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a clicked event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstRepliedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a replied event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope (always null for postmark — no reply tracking)

firstBouncedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of a bounced event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

firstUnsubscribedAt.description: First-occurrence (MIN) ISO 8601 timestamp of an unsubscribed event in this scope; null if it never happened in scope

byCampaign.description: Per-campaign breakdown — present only when brandId is provided without campaignId

campaign.description: Campaign-scoped status — present only when campaignId is provided

bounced.description: True if this email bounced anywhere in the org

unsubscribed.description: True if this email unsubscribed anywhere in the org

brandId.description: Brand ID — when provided without campaignId, returns per-campaign breakdown + aggregated brand status

campaignId.description: Campaign ID — when provided, returns campaign-scoped status (brandId is ignored)

get.summary: Get webhook URL for BYOK configuration
get.description: Returns the webhook URL that BYOK customers should paste into their Instantly dashboard webhook settings.

post.description: Verification: the campaign_id in the payload must exist in the database. Each campaign UUID is unguessable and stored with its org on creation.
Brand IDs, campaign ID, and workflow slug are read from headers (x-brand-id, x-campaign-id, x-workflow-slug) — do NOT pass them in the body.

post.summary: Dispatch reconcile webhook state against Instantly API
post.description:
Daily catch-up job that pulls Instantly's per-campaign state (aggregate, per-lead status, per-email records) and promotes any events missed by the webhook into the silver event log. Idempotent — safe to re-run.

Returns 202 immediately and runs the job in the background. Verify completion via Railway logs (`reconcile: done`) or by polling `instantly_*_raw` bronze tables.

Continuous worker that scans campaigns with `delivery_status='contacted'` stuck for >72h with no silver event proving Instantly ever sent. For each row: re-sends the lead onto a fresh healthy Instantly account, refunds the old cost rows, and provisions fresh costs against the new campaign. The row's local `delivery_status` stays `contacted` until a real `email_sent` webhook lands or it is terminally cancelled. The worker now runs continuously (not a daily cron) — this endpoint exists for legacy callers and is a no-op trigger.

get.summary: Get public-safe engagement latency for workflow slugs
get.description: Computes aggregate elapsed time from each recipient's first real send to first link click and first positive reply across the supplied workflow slugs. Returns only aggregate average, median, and sample size; no recipient, lead, campaign, or message data is exposed.

schema.description: Comma-separated workflow slugs to aggregate together.

parameters.description: Comma-separated workflow slugs to aggregate together.
parameters.name: workflowSlugs

post.summary: Get public-safe engagement latency for workflow slug groups
post.description: Computes aggregate elapsed time from each recipient's first real send to first link click and first positive reply for caller-owned workflow slug groups. Use group keys such as workflow dynasty slugs when the consumer owns dynasty metadata. Returns no per-recipient rows or campaign internals.

200.description: Engagement latency aggregates by caller-owned group

post.summary: Get stats grouped by sets of run IDs
post.description: Accepts named groups of run IDs and returns aggregated stats per group in a single call. Used by the leaderboard to fetch per-workflow stats.

post.summary: Transfer solo-brand rows from one org to another
post.description: Re-assigns org_id (and optionally brand_id) on all rows that reference exactly one brand matching sourceBrandId. When targetBrandId is present, also rewrites brand references to the target brand. Skips co-branding rows (multiple brand IDs). Idempotent.

post.summary: Set a manual reply qualification for a (campaign, lead) pair
post.description:
Record a human-set reply classification for a lead in a campaign. Used when Instantly's automatic webhook reply classification fails to detect a reply (e.g. the reply was sent to a non-leurre account that Instantly does not monitor).

**Bronze:** an `instantly_manual_qualifications_raw` row is appended for audit (append-only).

**Silver / Gold:** a corresponding row is inserted into `instantly_events` with `source='manual'`, so analytics counters (RepliesDetail) include the manual qualification alongside webhook events. `instantly_campaigns.reply_classification` is updated to the derived positive/negative/neutral value and `reply_classification_source` is set to `manual` so subsequent webhook events do not overwrite the human choice.

**Idempotence:** if the latest row for (org, campaign, lead) already has `status`, the call is a no-op — no new bronze row, no side effects. The response includes `idempotent: true` and the existing row.

get.summary: List manual reply qualifications (org-scoped audit history)
get.description: Returns the org's manual qualification history, sorted by `qualifiedAt` DESC. Optionally filter by `campaign_id` and/or `email`. Cross-org reads are blocked — only rows where `org_id` matches the request header are returned.

post.summary: Batch delivery status check for emails
post.description:
Batch delivery status check. Filters are in the body — headers are tracing/logging only.

**Modes:**
- **Brand mode** (`brandId` set, no `campaignId`): returns `byCampaign` (per-campaign breakdown keyed by campaign UUID), `brand` (aggregated), and `global`.
- **Campaign mode** (`campaignId` set, with or without `brandId`): returns `campaign` (single campaign status) and `global`. When both are provided, `brandId` is ignored.
- **Global only** (neither): returns only `global`.

**Aggregation rules for `brand`:**
- Boolean fields (`contacted`, `sent`, `delivered`, `opened`, `clicked`, `replied`, `bounced`, `unsubscribed`): `true` if true in at least one campaign (BOOL_OR).
- `replyClassification`: from the campaign with the most recent `lastDeliveredAt` that has a non-null classification.
- `lastDeliveredAt`: MAX across all campaigns.
- `firstContactedAt` / `firstSentAt` / `firstDeliveredAt` / `firstOpenedAt` / `firstClickedAt` / `firstRepliedAt` / `firstBouncedAt` / `firstUnsubscribedAt`: first-occurrence (MIN) timestamp of each event type in the scope, null if it never happened; brand = MIN across campaigns. Each agrees with its boolean (non-null iff the boolean is true; `firstDeliveredAt` consistent with `delivered = sent AND NOT bounced`).

**`global.email`** aggregates `bounced`/`unsubscribed` across ALL campaigns in the org, regardless of brand or campaign filters.

Fields not applicable to the active mode are always present but set to `null`.
